req.title: Joint account operations (boundary variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-043 defines three distinct test axes: (1) a primary joint-account holder performing a valid transaction (EP positive path), (2) a secondary holder attempting an ownership change which must yield 403 (NEG/DT error pathway), and (3) boundary value behaviour at exact min/max transaction thresholds on the /accounts/{id}/joint endpoint (BVA). A fourth STC test covers the state transition from an active joint account undergoing a valid operation to confirm the 'operation applied' outcome. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down account state, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_performs_valid_transaction
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Primary holder is authenticated and possesses a valid bearer token
Account balance is sufficient for the transaction amount
actions: Authenticate as the primary holder to obtain a bearer token
POST a transaction payload with a valid amount to /accounts/{id}/joint
Capture the HTTP response status and response body
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 status code is returned
Response body confirms the transaction was applied
Account balance reflects the deducted amount
trace: R-FIN-043: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header, assert_transaction_applied


def test_primary_holder_performs_valid_transaction(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    primary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["primary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>

    payload = {"amount": 100.00, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"}
    resp = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json=payload,
        headers=make_auth_header(primary_token),
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert_transaction_applied(body, expected_amount=100.00)

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Secondary holder is authenticated and possesses a valid bearer token
The ownership modification payload targets the primary holder field
actions: Authenticate as the secondary holder to obtain a bearer token
PATCH an ownership-change payload to /accounts/{id}/joint
Capture the HTTP response status and error body
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden status code is returned
Response body contains an error indicating insufficient permissions
trace: R-FIN-043: 'the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
R-FIN-043: 'Error pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header, get_current_ownership


def test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    secondary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["secondary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>

    original_ownership = get_current_ownership(client, account_id)

    payload = {"primary_holder_id": "attacker-user-id"}
    resp = client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json=payload,
        headers=make_auth_header(secondary_token),
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    post_ownership = get_current_ownership(client, account_id)
    assert post_ownership == original_ownership, "Ownership must not change"

tests.name: test_transaction_at_minimum_threshold_boundary
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary holder
System-defined minimum transaction amount is known (e.g. 0.01)
actions: Authenticate as the primary holder
POST a transaction with amount equal to the exact minimum threshold (0.01) to /accounts/{id}/joint
POST a transaction with amount one unit below minimum (0.00) to /accounts/{id}/joint
Capture both HTTP response statuses
expected: Transaction at exact minimum (0.01) returns HTTP 200 or 201 and is applied
Transaction below minimum (0.00) returns HTTP 400 or 422 and is rejected
trace: R-FIN-043: 'Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested'
R-FIN-043: 'Expected outcomes: operation applied' for valid threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header

MIN_AMOUNT = 0.01
BELOW_MIN_AMOUNT = 0.00


def test_transaction_at_minimum_threshold_boundary(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    primary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["primary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>
    headers = make_auth_header(primary_token)

    resp_min = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json={"amount": MIN_AMOUNT, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp_min.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Min boundary should be accepted, got {resp_min.status_code}"
    )

    resp_below = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json={"amount": BELOW_MIN_AMOUNT, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp_below.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Below-min boundary should be rejected, got {resp_below.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_transaction_at_maximum_threshold_boundary
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary holder
System-defined maximum single transaction amount is known (e.g. 1000000.00)
actions: Authenticate as the primary holder
POST a transaction with amount equal to the exact maximum threshold to /accounts/{id}/joint
POST a transaction with amount one unit above maximum to /accounts/{id}/joint
Capture both HTTP response statuses
expected: Transaction at exact maximum returns HTTP 200 or 201 and is applied
Transaction above maximum returns HTTP 400 or 422 and is rejected
trace: R-FIN-043: 'Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested'
R-FIN-043: 'Expected outcomes: operation applied' for valid threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header

MAX_AMOUNT = 1_000_000.00
ABOVE_MAX_AMOUNT = 1_000_000.01


def test_transaction_at_maximum_threshold_boundary(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    primary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["primary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>
    headers = make_auth_header(primary_token)

    resp_max = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json={"amount": MAX_AMOUNT, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp_max.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Max boundary should be accepted, got {resp_max.status_code}"
    )

    resp_above = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json={"amount": ABOVE_MAX_AMOUNT, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp_above.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Above-max boundary should be rejected, got {resp_above.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_joint_account_state_transition_after_transaction
preconditions: A joint account exists in ACTIVE state
actions: Read the current account state via GET /accounts/{id}/joint to confirm ACTIVE
POST a valid transaction as the primary holder
Read the account state again to verify the post-transaction state
expected: Transaction is accepted with HTTP 200 or 201
Post-transaction state remains ACTIVE with updated balance
Transaction record appears in account history
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header


def test_joint_account_state_transition_after_transaction(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    primary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["primary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>
    headers = make_auth_header(primary_token)

    pre_state = client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", headers=headers)
    assert pre_state.status_code == 200
    assert pre_state.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    pre_balance = pre_state.json()["balance"]

    txn_amount = 50.00
    resp = client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json={"amount": txn_amount, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)

    post_state = client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", headers=headers)
    assert post_state.status_code == 200
    body = post_state.json()
    assert body["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    assert abs(body["balance"] - (pre_balance - txn_amount)) < 0.001

tests.name: test_decision_table_holder_role_vs_operation_type
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Both holders have valid authentication tokens
Ownership-change and transaction operation payloads are available
actions: Primary holder attempts ownership change — expect 403
Secondary holder attempts a standard transaction — expect 200/201
Secondary holder attempts ownership change — expect 403
Primary holder attempts a standard transaction — expect 200/201
trace: R-FIN-043: 'secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
R-FIN-043: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions'
R-FIN-043: 'Error pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_auth_header


def test_decision_table_holder_role_vs_operation_type(<joint_account_fixture>):
    account_id = <joint_account_fixture>["account_id"]
    primary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["primary_token"]
    secondary_token = <joint_account_fixture>["secondary_token"]
    client = <api_client_fixture>
    ownership_payload = {"primary_holder_id": "new-owner-id"}
    txn_payload = {"amount": 10.00, "currency": "USD", "type": "debit"}
    url = f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint"

    r1 = client.patch(url, json=ownership_payload,
                      headers=make_auth_header(primary_token))
    assert r1.status_code == 403, f"Primary+ownership: expected 403, got {r1.status_code}"

    r2 = client.post(url, json=txn_payload,
                     headers=make_auth_header(secondary_token))
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 201), f"Secondary+txn: expected 200/201, got {r2.status_code}"

    r3 = client.patch(url, json=ownership_payload,
                      headers=make_auth_header(secondary_token))
    assert r3.status_code == 403, f"Secondary+ownership: expected 403, got {r3.status_code}"

    r4 = client.post(url, json=txn_payload,
                     headers=make_auth_header(primary_token))
    assert r4.status_code in (200, 201), f"Primary+txn: expected 200/201, got {r4.status_code}"
